pattern: Reversing the list turns 'delete every node with something bigger to its right' into 'delete every node smaller than the largest value seen so far while scanning left to right', which a single pass with a running maximum handles directly; reversing back restores the original order.
hints: A node survives only if every node after it is no larger -- that's easiest to check by looking from the right end of the list, not the left.
Reverse the list first. Now the question becomes: scanning left to right, keep a node only if it's at least as large as the biggest value you've seen so far.

Walk the reversed list keeping a running maximum; build a new list of only the nodes that are greater than or equal to that running maximum (updating it as you go), then reverse the result back to restore the original order.

edge_inputs.description: statement example, all values equal so nothing is removed
strictly increasing list, every node survives
strictly decreasing list, only the last node survives
the maximum sits in the middle, everything before it is removed but nothing after
two-node list where the second is smaller, first must be kept
two-node list where the second is larger, first must be removed

stress.description: random-length lists with random values, mixing increasing and decreasing runs
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 100000))
    vals = [rng.randint(1, 100000) for _ in range(n)]
    return [vals]

oracle_python:
class Solution:
    def removeNodes(self, head):
        vals = []
        node = head
        while node:
            vals.append(node.val)
            node = node.next
        n = len(vals)
        suffix_max = [0] * n
        running = float('-inf')
        for i in range(n - 1, -1, -1):
            running = max(running, vals[i])
            suffix_max[i] = running
        kept = [vals[i] for i in range(n) if i == n - 1 or vals[i] >= suffix_max[i + 1]]
        dummy = ListNode(0)
        tail = dummy
        for v in kept:
            tail.next = ListNode(v)
            tail = tail.next
        return dummy.next
